commands.review_summary:
The branch makes the iOS app universal by changing the XcodeGen target device family to iPhone+iPad and updates iOS docs/App Store metadata copy to mention iPad.

PR surface: Docs 0, Other 0. Total 0 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a broken existing-behavior report. The before state is source-provable on current main via `TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY: "1"`, and the PR body provides after-fix simulator proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The only remaining action is a maintainer release/distribution decision about simulator-only proof versus physical/TestFlight validation, not an automated repair.

target.title: feat(ios): support native iPad display
target.body:
## Summary

Makes the OpenClaw iOS app a **universal app** so it renders at the iPad's native resolution instead of running in scaled iPhone-compatibility mode. The only change is the `OpenClaw` app target's `TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY`: `"1"` → `"1,2"` in `apps/ios/project.yml` (xcodegen source of truth; the `pbxproj` is generated/gitignored).

Everything else required for native large-display support was already present, so no other changes were needed:
- `UILaunchScreen: {}` — resolution-independent launch (prerequisite for full-screen native sizing instead of letterboxing).
- `UISupportedInterfaceOrientations~ipad` — all four orientations already declared.
- Device logic (`DeviceInfoHelper`, `NodeDisplayName`) already handles the `.pad` idiom for gateway/node payloads.

Result: full-screen native rendering on the 13-inch iPad Air and 12.9/13-inch iPad Pro. The app is now also eligible for Split View / Slide Over (all orientations, no `UIRequiresFullScreen`).

## Verification

- **Build:** `xcodebuild -project OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPad Air 13-inch (M3)' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build` → `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` (SwiftFormat + SwiftLint prebuild phases passed).
- **Built `Info.plist`:** `UIDeviceFamily = {1, 2}`.
- **Runtime:** installed + launched on the iPad Air 13-inch simulator; screenshot captured at **2048×2732** — the full native panel resolution shared by the 13-inch iPad Air and 12.9/13-inch iPad Pro. A compatibility window would have produced a smaller scaled buffer. UI fills the full canvas (status bar full-width, full-width action buttons at the bottom edge); not letterboxed.

### Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: iOS app ran letterboxed/scaled (iPhone-compatibility mode) on iPad; now renders at native iPad resolution.
- Real environment tested: iOS Simulator — iPad Air 13-inch (M3), iOS 18, Debug.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `xcodegen generate`; `xcodebuild ... -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPad Air 13-inch (M3)' ... build`; `xcrun simctl install/launch`; `xcrun simctl io ... screenshot`.
- Evidence after fix: `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`; built `Info.plist` `UIDeviceFamily = {1, 2}`; screenshot 2048×2732 filling the display.
- Observed result after fix: app launches full-screen at native iPad resolution; UI adapts (SwiftUI), no letterboxing.
- What was not tested: physical iPad device; iPad Pro 12.9/13-inch simulator (same panel resolution as the Air 13-inch tested); Split View / Slide Over multitasking layouts; App Store/TestFlight iPad submission assets.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: empx2025
